title: Primary LightGBM Capacity Retune
summary: Retune the main LightGBM expert toward higher capacity and slower learning while preserving the current feature set, model panel, fold-wise blend, and fold-wise calibration.
feature_strategy: Keep the current feature pipeline unchanged: photometric colors and band-profile/extrema features, redshift transforms and bins, sky and galactic coordinate features, categorical crosses, covariate-only frequency/rank features, and locality-weighted SDSS17 auxiliary rows. No new features are introduced so the measured effect is attributable to the LightGBM training configuration.
baseline_model_panel: Use the current CatBoost, XGBoost, and LightGBM ensemble pipeline, but replace only the primary LightGBM hyperparameters: lower learning rate, more boosting rounds, larger num_leaves, slightly lower min_child_samples, higher feature/subsample fractions, and mild L1/L2 regularization.
model_panel_rationale: The current run showed LightGBM as the strongest standalone model, so a controlled LightGBM retune is the highest-upside single-model-family change while retaining CatBoost and XGBoost for diversity.
validation_strategy: Use the same 5-fold StratifiedKFold on competition rows, train auxiliary SDSS17 rows only inside each fold, optimize the existing fold-wise logit blend and fold-wise temperature/offset calibration on OOF validation slices, and report balanced accuracy on competition OOF rows only.
materialization_hint: Start from response.py. Change only LGBM_CONFIG to n_estimators=1150, learning_rate=0.035, num_leaves=191, min_child_samples=35, subsample=0.90, colsample_bytree=0.90, reg_alpha=0.02, reg_lambda=0.70; preserve all other feature engineering, folds, model families, weighting, blend search, calibration, and artifact writing.
expected_signal: If LightGBM was slightly underfit or benefited from more tree capacity, its OOF balanced accuracy and its fold-wise blend weights should increase, producing a small gain over the parent 0.968002. If the score drops, the parent LightGBM setting was already near the regularization/capacity optimum.
risk: The deeper LightGBM setting may overfit or increase runtime, especially if the current improvement is mostly from calibration rather than single-model capacity. Because all downstream aggregation is unchanged, any score movement should be easy to attribute.
rationale:
Hypothesis 000030: Retune the main LightGBM expert toward higher capacity and slower learning while preserving the current feature set, model panel, fold-wise blend, and fold-wise calibration.
Parent pipeline score reference: 0.968002 OOF balanced accuracy.
This experiment is intentionally atomic so the score movement can be attributed to the named change.
implementation_hint: Start from response.py. Change only LGBM_CONFIG to n_estimators=1150, learning_rate=0.035, num_leaves=191, min_child_samples=35, subsample=0.90, colsample_bytree=0.90, reg_alpha=0.02, reg_lambda=0.70; preserve all other feature engineering, folds, model families, weighting, blend search, calibration, and artifact writing.
expected_effect: If LightGBM was slightly underfit or benefited from more tree capacity, its OOF balanced accuracy and its fold-wise blend weights should increase, producing a small gain over the parent 0.968002. If the score drops, the parent LightGBM setting was already near the regularization/capacity optimum.

parent_cv_reference.note: Use this hypothesis run score, not the parent score, as the empirical result.
